question.label: Surprised late
question.name: When stakeholders feel surprised late, what gap does that point to in your approach?
question.description: Turns the evidence she named into a plainly stated performance gap around stakeholder engagement and visibility.

answer: I need a tighter narrative on tradeoffs, not just stronger models.

assessment.note: She names a specific gap — weak tradeoff narrative versus technical strength — which is strong clarity and mild ownership signal.

new_queue.label: Tradeoff narrative
new_queue.name: What makes the tradeoff narrative hard to state earlier?

new_queue.label: Escalation judgment
new_queue.name: What kind of call did you hold too long before escalating this quarter?

new_queue.label: Next-quarter focus
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, what do you want to work on first over the next quarter?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 6)

history.answer_excerpt: I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I need a tighter narrative on tradeoffs, not just stronger models.
I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I get work done, yet I am not consistently shaping decisions early.
Some feedback has been about being clearer with non-technical partners.
I tend to wait until analysis is complete before socializing direction.
That slows trust because stakeholders feel surprised late.
